author: ada
text: The benchmark is useful, but the test set favors short-lived command line workloads. A service workload would expose different latency and memory tradeoffs.

author: ben
text: That matches our production experience. Tail latency rose after the migration even though median latency improved, so both distributions should be reported.

author: cy
text: The repository includes the raw measurements and a reproducible harness, which makes this limitation straightforward to test.

author: dev
text: A second concern is operational complexity. The proposed design adds two failure domains and requires coordinated upgrades, while the article only measures steady-state throughput.

author: eli
text: There is a simpler deployment described in the appendix, but it gives up the isolation benefit that motivates the design.

author: fox
text:
> The benchmark is useful, but the test set favors short-lived command line workloads.
> A service workload would expose different latency and memory tradeoffs.
I agree.
